summary: No-ship: P-6 remains acceptance-bypassable. The RED lock, committed assertion, and verify-record are valid; no separate new critical issue was found. Simpler alternative: exact-match the complete `PassGuard::begin` body instead of using a file-wide substring. Open question: none.

findings.title: W8(l₁) permits a test-only outer branch around the approved call
findings.body: W8(l₁) only counts `recover_graves(` and checks that the normalized file contains the approved call statement. That exact statement can remain inside an enclosing `if cfg!(test)` or hook-present branch while the other branch calls a differently named raw helper. The count and substring still pass, W11 selects the correct branch, and production selects the broken path that aborts when a grave vanishes. Therefore the claimed unconditional delegation is not enforced and M16/M17 remain alive.
findings.recommendation: Extract and exact-normalize the complete `PassGuard::begin` declaration and body, including surrounding attributes and binding context. Add call-site variants with an outer `cfg(test)` and hook-presence branch to M16/M17.

next_steps: Replace W8(l₁)'s global count/contains assertions with complete-body equality.
Re-review only P-6 after adding the surrounding-branch mutants; keep the approved B+A design unchanged.
